{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Finance & Inventory Manager",
  "description": "Auldon Limited is a toy trading company strategically located in the commercial centre of idumota Lagos Nigeria, it is a wholesale distribution organization servicing major departmental stores, Multi-National Organizations and educational institutions within Nigeria and its sub-region. Founded as Arigold Toys in 1996 before transforming into a limited liability company, with the registration RC-439491 and named auldon limited in the year 2002.

Auldon limited is famously known internationally for pioneering the African Themed Unity Girls Dolls, a collection of 14-inch dolls, each representing Nigeria&rsquo;s three major tribes which promotes cultural inclusion amongst Africans and her diaspora communities. The product is being created to deliver an empowering social message to girls around Nigeria and the world at large.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance &amp; Inventory Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Description&nbsp;


	We are currently recruiting for a Finance and Inventory Manager to oversee thefinancial operations and inventory control of our retail store.
	The goal is to enable thecompany&rsquo;s leaders to make sound business decisions and meet the company&rsquo;s objectives.
	In this role, you will have the opportunity to utilize your expertise in financial analysis andstrategic planning. By analyzing day-to-day financial activities with precision, you will provide valuable insights and recommendations to upper management.
	Your guidance will empower the company&rsquo;s leaders to make informed decisions that align with theorganization&rsquo;s long-term goals.


Key Responsibilities


	Analyze daily sales reports and financial statements for trends and discrepancies.
	Prepare and present financial performance reports to senior management
	Oversee operations of the finance department, set goals and objectives, anddesign a framework for these to be met.
	Oversee inventory management and stock control processes
	Minimize stock loss and ensure accurate inventory records
	Ensuring all finance and accounting operations comply with generally acceptedaccounting principles and federal and state laws and regulations.
	Ensuring day-to-day transactions such as accounts payable, cash receipts andaccounts receivable, credit card transactions, and payroll are recordedproperly.
	Performing fiscal year-end close activities and assisting with activities and&nbsp;requirements of external auditors.
	Review and approve expenditure requests within designated limits.
	Oversee reconciliation of general ledger accounts and bank statements


Required Skills


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in a finance or accounting role, with at least 2 yearsin a managerial position.
	Proven experience in retail finance or a similar industry.
	Strong knowledge of financial reporting, budgeting, and forecasting.
	Experience in cash flow management and financial modeling.
	Familiarity with accounting software and enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems.
	Ability to prepare and present comprehensive financial reports to senior management.
	Strong understanding of inventory management and cost-reduction strategies.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, especially Excel.
	Experience in preparing monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements.
	Familiarity with tax filing requirements and coordination with tax advisors
	Certified Accountant (CPA), Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA), or ICAN is compulsory.


Salary
N400,000 - N450,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified&nbsp;candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;Operations@auldontoys.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-20",
  "validThrough": "2026-04-04",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Auldon Limited"
    
  },

Finance & Inventory Manager Job at Auldon Limited




        
    

        
        Auldon Limited is a toy trading company strategically located in the commercial centre of idumota Lagos Nigeria, it is a wholesale distribution organization servicing major departmental stores, Multi-National Organizations and educational institutions within Nigeria and its sub-region. Founded as Arigold Toys in 1996 before transforming into a limited liability company, with the registration RC-439491 and named auldon limited in the year 2002.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Auldon limited is famously known internationally for pioneering the African Themed Unity Girls Dolls, a collection of 14-inch dolls, each representing Nigeria’s three major tribes which promotes cultural inclusion amongst Africans and her diaspora communities. The product is being created to deliver an empowering social message to girls around Nigeria and the world at large.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance & Inventory Manager

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Description 


	We are currently recruiting for a Finance and Inventory Manager to oversee thefinancial operations and inventory control of our retail store.
	The goal is to enable thecompany’s leaders to make sound business decisions and meet the company’s objectives.
	In this role, you will have the opportunity to utilize your expertise in financial analysis andstrategic planning. By analyzing day-to-day financial activities with precision, you will provide valuable insights and recommendations to upper management.
	Your guidance will empower the company’s leaders to make informed decisions that align with theorganization’s long-term goals.


Key Responsibilities


	Analyze daily sales reports and financial statements for trends and discrepancies.
	Prepare and present financial performance reports to senior management
	Oversee operations of the finance department, set goals and objectives, anddesign a framework for these to be met.
	Oversee inventory management and stock control processes
	Minimize stock loss and ensure accurate inventory records
	Ensuring all finance and accounting operations comply with generally acceptedaccounting principles and federal and state laws and regulations.
	Ensuring day-to-day transactions such as accounts payable, cash receipts andaccounts receivable, credit card transactions, and payroll are recordedproperly.
	Performing fiscal year-end close activities and assisting with activities and requirements of external auditors.
	Review and approve expenditure requests within designated limits.
	Oversee reconciliation of general ledger accounts and bank statements


Required Skills


	Bachelor’s Degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in a finance or accounting role, with at least 2 yearsin a managerial position.
	Proven experience in retail finance or a similar industry.
	Strong knowledge of financial reporting, budgeting, and forecasting.
	Experience in cash flow management and financial modeling.
	Familiarity with accounting software and enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems.
	Ability to prepare and present comprehensive financial reports to senior management.
	Strong understanding of inventory management and cost-reduction strategies.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, especially Excel.
	Experience in preparing monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements.
	Familiarity with tax filing requirements and coordination with tax advisors
	Certified Accountant (CPA), Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA), or ICAN is compulsory.


Salary
N400,000 - N450,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Medical Records / Administrative Officer",
  "description": "Open Hospital Limited, your health is our priority. Your comfort is our commitment. Quality Healthcare You Can Trust. Compassion You Can Feel. A privately owned healthcare facility under the Open Health Foundation, providing accessible, patient centered medical services for individuals and families in Abuja, Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Medical Records / Administrative Officer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a detail-oriented and organized Medical Records / Administrative Officer to manage patient records, support administrative functions, and ensure smooth operation of the healthcare facility.
	The successful candidate will maintain accurate records, assist with reporting, and coordinate administrative tasks to enhance patient care and facility efficiency.


Key Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate and up-to-date patient medical records.
	File, retrieve, and manage both physical and electronic health records.
	Ensure confidentiality and compliance with medical records regulations.
	Assist in scheduling appointments and managing patient flow.
	Support administrative tasks such as correspondence, reporting, and data entry.
	Coordinate between departments for smooth operational workflow.
	Prepare reports on patient statistics, admissions, and discharge summaries.
	Handle inquiries from patients, staff, and external organizations.
	Ensure compliance with hospital policies, data protection laws, and quality standards.


Requirements


	Diploma, Degree, or Certificate in Health Information Management, Administration, or related field.
	Knowledge of medical records management systems and procedures.
	Strong organizational and attention-to-detail skills.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work efficiently under pressure and meet deadlines.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office or hospital management software.


Preferred Qualifications:


	1&ndash;3 years of experience in medical records or healthcare administration.
	Familiarity with electronic medical records (EMR) systems.
	Knowledge of healthcare regulations and patient confidentiality standards.

Medical Records / Administrative Officer Job at Open Hospital Limited




        
    

        
        Open Hospital Limited, your health is our priority. Your comfort is our commitment. Quality Healthcare You Can Trust. Compassion You Can Feel. A privately owned healthcare facility under the Open Health Foundation, providing accessible, patient centered medical services for individuals and families in Abuja, Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Medical Records / Administrative Officer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a detail-oriented and organized Medical Records / Administrative Officer to manage patient records, support administrative functions, and ensure smooth operation of the healthcare facility.
	The successful candidate will maintain accurate records, assist with reporting, and coordinate administrative tasks to enhance patient care and facility efficiency.


Key Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate and up-to-date patient medical records.
	File, retrieve, and manage both physical and electronic health records.
	Ensure confidentiality and compliance with medical records regulations.
	Assist in scheduling appointments and managing patient flow.
	Support administrative tasks such as correspondence, reporting, and data entry.
	Coordinate between departments for smooth operational workflow.
	Prepare reports on patient statistics, admissions, and discharge summaries.
	Handle inquiries from patients, staff, and external organizations.
	Ensure compliance with hospital policies, data protection laws, and quality standards.


Requirements


	Diploma, Degree, or Certificate in Health Information Management, Administration, or related field.
	Knowledge of medical records management systems and procedures.
	Strong organizational and attention-to-detail skills.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work efficiently under pressure and meet deadlines.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office or hospital management software.


Preferred Qualifications:


	1–3 years of experience in medical records or healthcare administration.
	Familiarity with electronic medical records (EMR) systems.
	Knowledge of healthcare regulations and patient confidentiality standards.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Lead",
  "description": "Zagus Power Systems Limited is a trusted provider of advanced Power Quality, Backup, and Electrical infrastructure solutions in Nigeria. We serve residential, commercial, and industrial clients with world-class products and services. As the exclusive distributor of Zektron products in Nigeria, Zagus Power Systems Limited has supplied, installed, and maintained critical power solutions across the country.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Lead

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Power Systems/RenewableEnergy

Job Summary


	The Business Development Manager is responsible for identifying and developing strategic business relationships with potential customers, working closely with the Managing Director and overseeing the onsite operation.
	Similarly, as the Business Development Manager, you will take the lead in the development of new business opportunities that support Zagus Power System Ltd., overall growth strategy. We are seeking someone with an entrepreneurial spirit, a passion for sales, and a proven track record of success, who takes a hands-on approach to building business opportunities.
	In this key position, you will work closely with marketing, sales, and technical teams to achieve established sales goals and implement business development initiatives that maximize and growZagus Power System Ltd.,footprint and generate bottom-line revenue for the company. This position reports directly to the head of Business development.


Responsibilities
Core duties and responsibilities include the following:


	Develop new business opportunities with potential customers at all stages of the sales cycle including targeting, prospecting, and presenting compelling business propositions.
	Prepare and present powerful, persuasive sales presentations that effectively demonstrate the value proposition ofZagus Power System Ltd.,.
	Develop and maintain a robust deal pipeline toward targeted entities to continuously grow the business and generate sales.
	Effectively manage large-scale, complex contract negotiations and close new business deals with the customers, working closely with the head of business development.
	Maintain consistent sales growth by continuously establishing new accounts and maintaining existing accounts.
	Develop and execute a strategic business plan that meets or exceeds established sales goals and supports Company revenue and profitability targets.
	Attend conferences and professional association meetings and promoteZagus Power System Ltd.
	Prepare and give business reviews and presentations to the senior management team on a monthly basis.
	ToIdentify and pursue new business opportunities through targeted prospecting, cold calling, and networking within sectors like telecommunications, healthcare, and data centers.
	To Collaboratte with technical teams to design bespoke energy solutions and preparing persuasive commercial bids and tenders.
	Maintain deep knowledge of the competitive landscape for UPS, inverters and Batteries products to refine the company&#39;s value proposition.
	To Oversee the entire sales process from initial contact and negotiation to deal closure and client handover.


Job Requirements


	Education: Minimum of Bachelor&#39;s Degree inElectrical/Electronic Engineering, Physics, with an MBA , BSC in Marketing, Economics or other related discipline.
	Industry Experience: Typically requires 3-5yearsof experience in technical B2B sales or business development specifically within the power, UPS, or renewable energy sectors.
	Technical Acumen: Strong understanding of power backup systems, battery storage (e.g., Lithium-ion), solar inverters, and voltage stabilization technology.
	Soft Skills: Advanced negotiation, presentation, and relationship-management skills, with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to non-technical audiences.
	Tools: Proficiency inCRM software(like Salesforce or HubSpot), Microsoft Office Suite, and Google Workplace.
	Mobility: A willingness to travel regionally for site visits and client meetings.


Salary
N250,000 - N400,000 Net

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@zaguspower.com&nbsp;using &quot;Business Development Lead, Lagos&quot;.as the subject of the email.",

Business Development Lead Job at Zagus Power Systems Limited




        
    

        
        Zagus Power Systems Limited is a trusted provider of advanced Power Quality, Backup, and Electrical infrastructure solutions in Nigeria. We serve residential, commercial, and industrial clients with world-class products and services. As the exclusive distributor of Zektron products in Nigeria, Zagus Power Systems Limited has supplied, installed, and maintained critical power solutions across the country.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Lead

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Power Systems/RenewableEnergy

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Business Development Manager is responsible for identifying and developing strategic business relationships with potential customers, working closely with the Managing Director and overseeing the onsite operation.
	Similarly, as the Business Development Manager, you will take the lead in the development of new business opportunities that support Zagus Power System Ltd., overall growth strategy. We are seeking someone with an entrepreneurial spirit, a passion for sales, and a proven track record of success, who takes a hands-on approach to building business opportunities.
	In this key position, you will work closely with marketing, sales, and technical teams to achieve established sales goals and implement business development initiatives that maximize and growZagus Power System Ltd.,footprint and generate bottom-line revenue for the company. This position reports directly to the head of Business development.


Responsibilities
Core duties and responsibilities include the following:


	Develop new business opportunities with potential customers at all stages of the sales cycle including targeting, prospecting, and presenting compelling business propositions.
	Prepare and present powerful, persuasive sales presentations that effectively demonstrate the value proposition ofZagus Power System Ltd.,.
	Develop and maintain a robust deal pipeline toward targeted entities to continuously grow the business and generate sales.
	Effectively manage large-scale, complex contract negotiations and close new business deals with the customers, working closely with the head of business development.
	Maintain consistent sales growth by continuously establishing new accounts and maintaining existing accounts.
	Develop and execute a strategic business plan that meets or exceeds established sales goals and supports Company revenue and profitability targets.
	Attend conferences and professional association meetings and promoteZagus Power System Ltd.
	Prepare and give business reviews and presentations to the senior management team on a monthly basis.
	ToIdentify and pursue new business opportunities through targeted prospecting, cold calling, and networking within sectors like telecommunications, healthcare, and data centers.
	To Collaboratte with technical teams to design bespoke energy solutions and preparing persuasive commercial bids and tenders.
	Maintain deep knowledge of the competitive landscape for UPS, inverters and Batteries products to refine the company's value proposition.
	To Oversee the entire sales process from initial contact and negotiation to deal closure and client handover.


Job Requirements


	Education: Minimum of Bachelor's Degree inElectrical/Electronic Engineering, Physics, with an MBA , BSC in Marketing, Economics or other related discipline.
	Industry Experience: Typically requires 3-5yearsof experience in technical B2B sales or business development specifically within the power, UPS, or renewable energy sectors.
	Technical Acumen: Strong understanding of power backup systems, battery storage (e.g., Lithium-ion), solar inverters, and voltage stabilization technology.
	Soft Skills: Advanced negotiation, presentation, and relationship-management skills, with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to non-technical audiences.
	Tools: Proficiency inCRM software(like Salesforce or HubSpot), Microsoft Office Suite, and Google Workplace.
	Mobility: A willingness to travel regionally for site visits and client meetings.


Salary
N250,000 - N400,000 Net

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Business Development Lead, Lagos".as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Intelligence Officer",
  "description": "Avetium Consult&nbsp;is a Business Process Outsourcing and Technology solution company incorporated in Nigeria.&nbsp;Avetium Consult&nbsp;is what happens when top experts of top multinationals from diverse fields and similar values come together to deliver transformational solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Intelligence Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (Hybrid)
Function: Enterprise Marketing &ndash; Corporate &amp; Public Sector
Duration: 3&ndash;6 months (Internship)

Key Responsibilities
Performance Monitoring &amp; BI Collaboration:


	Work closely with the Business Intelligence (BI) team to monitor daily, weekly, and monthly product and segment performance.
	Review dashboards, reports, and performance trackers to identify gaps, anomalies, and underperformance.
	Catalogue performance issues and observations in a structured and traceable manner.


Issue Tracking &amp; Follow-through:


	Log issues identified through BI reports, customer feedback, and internal reviews.
	Actively follow up with relevant stakeholders (product, sales, IT, support, finance, etc.) to drive resolution.
	Track issues to closure and provide regular progress updates.


Insights &amp; Analysis:


	Collate insights from customers, account teams, support teams, and internal meetings.
	Translate qualitative feedback and quantitative data into concise insight summaries.
	Support recommendations for product and engagement improvements.


Reporting &amp; Visibility:


	Support preparation of daily, weekly, and monthly performance updates.
	Maintain simple dashboards, trackers, and action logs.
	Highlight risks, trends, and exceptions requiring escalation.


Documentation &amp; Knowledge Management:


	Prepare meeting notes, summaries, and presentations.
	Maintain organised documentation of segment performance and resolutions.
	Support preparation of internal reviews and planning sessions.


General Support:


	Assist with ad-hoc analysis, presentations, and special projects.
	Support planning and preparation for customer engagements.


Required Skills &amp; Competencies


	Undergraduate or recent graduate in Business, Economics, Engineering, Data/Analytics, Marketing, or related discipline
	Exposure to BI tools or reporting is an advantage
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
	High attention to detail and follow-through
	Strong organisational and documentation skills
	Good written and verbal communication skills
	Comfortable working with data, reports and presentations
	Proficient in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, collaboration tools (e.g. Teams).
	Knowledge of BI dashboards and internal performance trackers.
	Structured mentoring and regular check-ins with the segment team.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-01",

  },

Business Intelligence Officer Job at Avetium Consult




        
    

        
        Avetium Consult is a Business Process Outsourcing and Technology solution company incorporated in Nigeria. Avetium Consult is what happens when top experts of top multinationals from diverse fields and similar values come together to deliver transformational solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Intelligence Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (Hybrid)
Function: Enterprise Marketing – Corporate & Public Sector
Duration: 3–6 months (Internship)

Key Responsibilities
Performance Monitoring & BI Collaboration:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Work closely with the Business Intelligence (BI) team to monitor daily, weekly, and monthly product and segment performance.
	Review dashboards, reports, and performance trackers to identify gaps, anomalies, and underperformance.
	Catalogue performance issues and observations in a structured and traceable manner.


Issue Tracking & Follow-through:


	Log issues identified through BI reports, customer feedback, and internal reviews.
	Actively follow up with relevant stakeholders (product, sales, IT, support, finance, etc.) to drive resolution.
	Track issues to closure and provide regular progress updates.


Insights & Analysis:


	Collate insights from customers, account teams, support teams, and internal meetings.
	Translate qualitative feedback and quantitative data into concise insight summaries.
	Support recommendations for product and engagement improvements.


Reporting & Visibility:


	Support preparation of daily, weekly, and monthly performance updates.
	Maintain simple dashboards, trackers, and action logs.
	Highlight risks, trends, and exceptions requiring escalation.


Documentation & Knowledge Management:


	Prepare meeting notes, summaries, and presentations.
	Maintain organised documentation of segment performance and resolutions.
	Support preparation of internal reviews and planning sessions.


General Support:


	Assist with ad-hoc analysis, presentations, and special projects.
	Support planning and preparation for customer engagements.


Required Skills & Competencies


	Undergraduate or recent graduate in Business, Economics, Engineering, Data/Analytics, Marketing, or related discipline
	Exposure to BI tools or reporting is an advantage
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
	High attention to detail and follow-through
	Strong organisational and documentation skills
	Good written and verbal communication skills
	Comfortable working with data, reports and presentations
	Proficient in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, collaboration tools (e.g. Teams).
	Knowledge of BI dashboards and internal performance trackers.
	Structured mentoring and regular check-ins with the segment team.
